item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Providers Microsoft Storage Checknameavailability

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.

request.description: Checks that the storage account name is valid and is not already in use.

response.name: OK -- Operation to check the storage account name availability was successful.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.

request.description: Lists all the storage accounts available under the subscription. Note that storage keys are not returned; use the ListKeys operation for this.

response.name: OK -- List of storage accounts was retrieved and returned successfully.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Providers Microsoft Storage Usages

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.

request.description: Gets the current usage count and the limit for the resources under the subscription.

response.name: OK -- current usage account and limit retrieved and returned successfully.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts Accountname Listkeys

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: Lists the access keys for the specified storage account.

response.name: OK -- list of keys retrieved and returned successfully.

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: Regenerates one of the access keys for the specified storage account.

(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: List SAS credentials of a storage account.

response.name: OK -- returned the account SAS created for the storage account requested.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts Accountname Listservicesas

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: List service SAS credentials of a specific resource.

response.name: OK -- returned the service SAS created for the storage service requested.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Put Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts Accountname

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: Asynchronously creates a new storage account with the specified parameters. If an account is already created and a subsequent create request is issued with different properties, the account properties will be updated. If an account is already created and a subsequent create or update request is issued with the exact same set of properties, the request will succeed.

response.name: OK -- returned when the storage account was already created from a previous request with the same properties specified in the request body.
Accepted -- Create or update request accepted; operation will complete asynchronously.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Delete Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts Accountname

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: Deletes a storage account in Microsoft Azure.

response.name: NoContent -- account does not exist in the subscription.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts Accountname

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: Returns the properties for the specified storage account including but not limited to name, SKU name, location, and account status. The ListKeys operation should be used to retrieve storage keys.

response.name: OK -- properties retrieved successfully for the storage account.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Patch Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts Accountname

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The name of the storage account within the specified resource group. Storage account names must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only.

request.description: The update operation can be used to update the SKU, encryption, access tier, or tags for a storage account. It can also be used to map the account to a custom domain. Only one custom domain is supported per storage account; the replacement/change of custom domain is not supported. In order to replace an old custom domain, the old value must be cleared/unregistered before a new value can be set. The update of multiple properties is supported. This call does not change the storage keys for the account. If you want to change the storage account keys, use the regenerate keys operation. The location and name of the storage account cannot be changed after creation.

response.name: OK -- storage account properties updated successfully.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storage Storageaccounts

variable.description: (Required) Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify the Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.
(Required) The name of the resource group within the user's subscription. The name is case insensitive.

request.description: Lists all the storage accounts available under the given resource group. Note that storage keys are not returned; use the ListKeys operation for this.

response.name: OK -- List of storage accounts in the given resource group retrieved and returned successfully.
